title: Growth of Functions Quiz (Warmup)
instructions: Answer each question. MCQs are auto-graded. Short answers are graded by Claude (default) or self-grading.

questions.prompt: Which grows faster for sufficiently large $n$?
A single loop from $1..n$ with $O(1)$ body is:
Two nested loops each running $n$ times usually give:
Recursive factorial uses $O(n)$ extra space mainly because of:
For Maximum Subsequence Sum, the linear-time method is commonly:
In 1-2 sentences, explain the difference between $O(g(n))$ and $\Theta(g(n))$.
questions.expected: $O(g(n))$ says $T(n)$ grows no faster than a constant multiple of $g(n)$; $\Theta(g(n))$ says $T(n)$ grows at the same asymptotic rate (both upper and lower bounded by $g(n)$).

questions.prompt: Suppose an algorithm does $O(n)$ preprocessing, then an $O(n^2)$ phase. What is total asymptotic runtime and why?
questions.expected: $O(n^2)$. The sum $O(n)+O(n^2)$ is dominated by the larger term.

questions.prompt: For array [-2, 11, -4, 13, -5, -2], what is the maximum subsequence sum?
